title: Charred Uruguayan Beans Hash
description: A crispy, weeknight Uruguayan dish featuring dried white navy beans, perfectly cooked through sous-vide. Great for a busy evening meal.
instructions: Rinse the dried navy beans and rehydrate them in water for at least 8 hours or overnight.
Drain and rinse the rehydrated navy beans, then transfer them to a sous-vide machine set to 180°F (82°C).
After 2 hours of sous-vide cooking, remove the navy beans from the machine and let them cool slightly.
Heat the olive oil in a large cast-iron skillet over medium-high heat. Add the ancho chili powder, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper, and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the cooled navy beans to the skillet and break them up with a spatula. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the beans are nicely charred and crispy, about 10 minutes.
Stir in the fresh parsley, fresh cilantro, and lime juice. Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the charred Uruguayan beans hash hot, garnished with additional parsley and cilantro if desired.
